stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML topics like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The presence of 'llama' indicates relevance to the latest generation of large language models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The model is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft has identified a new malware strain called Crypto Clipper.
The malware is designed to steal cryptocurrency and spreads via USB drives.
Crypto Clipper utilizes Tor for its communication, suggesting advanced evasion techniques.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: The research identifies that fine-tuning language models on insecure code can cause emergent misalignment due to poorly understood internal structures.
The study proposes and validates an 'actionable activation-space direction' capable of detecting this misalignment across multiple model architectures.
This direction achieved a high separation rate (99.6%) between aligned and misaligned activations in four different instruction-tuned model families.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with key technologies like transformers and safetensors, indicating its technical nature.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies such as transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline focuses on text-generation capabilities of the model.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements translate to higher quality model outputs and faster response times.
The updated engine also demonstrates reduced memory consumption when running models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now integrates MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework.
This integration aims to provide the fastest performance for running Ollama on Apple Silicon.
The feature is currently available in a preview release.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using both local and cloud models.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: The Atlantic has made four datasets of music used to train AI models publicly searchable.
Two of the discovered music datasets are massive, containing 12 million and 9 million tracks respectively.
This development highlights the transparency and scale of data fueling modern AI training.

stories.title: The UK will scan asylum-seekers’ faces for age checks—despite knowing the tech is flawed
stories.author: Matt Burgess, Maddy Varner, May Bulman, Gabriel Geiger, WIRED.com
bullet_points: The UK government plans to use facial scanning technology for age verification of asylum seekers.
Independent tests have demonstrated significant risks of life-altering errors with the specific age-verification technology being used.
The implementation proceeds despite known flaws in the technology, raising major ethical and accuracy concerns.

stories.title: FDA advisors unanimously vote to approve Moderna's mRNA after agency drama
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: The FDA advisors unanimously approved Moderna's mRNA vaccine.
The approval followed a period of 'agency drama,' including a refusal by a Trump official to review the vaccine.
This represents a significant milestone in the regulatory approval process for mRNA technology.

stories.title: As China looms, Taiwan makes more drones for defense and the US military
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Taiwan is increasing its drone production for national defense.
The increased defense spending in Taiwan is expected to have positive economic spillover effects overseas.
The development is framed against the backdrop of geopolitical tensions involving China.

stories.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
stories.author: Nityanand Mathur
bullet_points: FlowEdit addresses the static nature of high-quality flow-matching TTS models, which fail on out-of-vocabulary words post-deployment.
It introduces a lifelong adaptation framework that learns pronunciation corrections via latent conditioning edits instead of full model retraining.
The method optimizes token-level perturbations within the text embedding space when corrective feedback is provided.

stories.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
stories.author: Jana Diesner
bullet_points: Introduces 'StylisticBias,' a new benchmark to evaluate attribute-level social bias in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses the critical gap in understanding how visual cues influence MLLM judgments about people in consequential settings.
The benchmark uses controlled photorealistic faces to separate appearance effects from inherent identity differences.

stories.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
stories.author: Hamed Zamani
bullet_points: Proposes a novel LLM alignment technique using implicit user feedback instead of relying solely on expensive explicit human feedback.
Addresses two key limitations of current alignment methods: the rarity of explicit feedback and the failure to utilize valuable implicit user signals.
Leverages implicit feedback, drawing a parallel to the data advantages held by major internet platforms.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes a 'local-first' approach, allowing AI to run on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Polymarket reportedly paid people to post fake videos of themselves placing bets
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Polymarket, a prediction market platform, is reportedly paying users to create deceptive content.
The scheme involves filming fake bets and celebrating fabricated wins on social media.
The Wall Street Journal investigation uncovered over 1,100 such deceptive clips.

stories.title: NASA asks Northrop Grumman to stop working on lunar HALO module
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA has directed Northrop Grumman to halt work on the lunar HALO module.
The decision involves reassigning the majority of the affected employees to other ongoing programs.
This signals a potential shift or change in NASA's lunar mission priorities or contractor assignments.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The research investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in AI.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Sudarshan Kamath
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of clarity regarding how individual words influence the acoustic output in style-captioned Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
It introduces cross-attention attribution, adapting the DAAM framework to the speech domain for the first time, to analyze this influence.
The method generates per-token heatmaps across multiple layers of speech diffusion models to improve controllability in expressive TTS.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to manage structured task states for AI agents.
Addresses a key limitation in current agents where state information (facts, constraints) is implicitly reconstructed from the prompt across turns.
Focuses specifically on improving policy adherence and state management in complex domains like customer service.

stories.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
stories.author: Fengxiang He
bullet_points: DeepSWIP addresses the limitation of standard associational inference in neurosymbolic systems like DeepProbLog.
It introduces a single-world counterfactual semantics to enable causal reasoning and interventions within these systems.
The method utilizes neural materialization to translate fixed-context neural predicates into standard ProbLog choices for counterfactual computation.

stories.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
stories.author: Georgia Channing
bullet_points: Introduces SARLO-80, a new large-scale dataset designed to bridge the gap between optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data.
Addresses a critical limitation in multimodal AI by providing high-resolution SAR data that preserves complex-valued measurements and native acquisition geometry.
Aims to enable more physically grounded multimodal learning for foundation models, moving beyond current low-resolution, intensity-only SAR benchmarks.

stories.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
stories.author: Dmitrii Babaev
bullet_points: The paper introduces Multi-LCB, an extension of the LiveCodeBench (LCB) benchmark.
LCB is a recognized benchmark for evaluating LLMs on code generation, known for its contamination-aware design.
Multi-LCB addresses LCB's limitation by expanding evaluation capabilities beyond Python to multiple programming languages.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM that operates significantly in a continuous latent space.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Introduces 'privacy via predictability' as a new framework to measure privacy leakage.
Addresses the limitations of traditional Differential Privacy (DP) by moving beyond worst-case guarantees.
Explicitly incorporates attacker knowledge, compromised data portions, and query families into the privacy model.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in LLM serving systems by focusing on low-latency, small-batch, on-device scenarios, contrasting with high-throughput KV caching.
Introduces 'Execution-State Capsules' for checkpointing and restoring the full execution state, not just the KV cache.
Targets use cases like interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies that require frequent branching and tight responsiveness budgets.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation of LLM coding agents: their lack of high-level operational knowledge about a software repository.
The paper investigates the effectiveness of using supplementary context files (like AGENTS.md) to guide coding agents.
It proposes a 'Probe-and-Refine' tuning method to improve agent performance by incorporating repository guidance.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to manage structured task states for policy-adherent AI agents.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where task state (facts, constraints, conditions) must be reconstructed from the prompt each turn.
Focuses on improving reliability in complex domains like customer service by explicitly tracking state across tool calls and policy adherence.

stories.title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
stories.author: Thomas Brox
bullet_points: Introduces RefRad2D, a large-scale bilingual (German/English) dataset (1.2M pairs) for radiology image-text tasks.
Proposes RefRad2D to enable training of Spatially Grounded Vision-Language Models (VLMs) without manual spatial annotations.
The model, RadGrounder, jointly performs report generation, visual question answering (VQA), and spatial grounding using LLM-based curation.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the intersection of hate speech and misinformation, a gap in current NLP research.
Proposes a new dataset ('CATCH-ME') to provide high-quality, contextually annotated examples for training LLMs.
Aims to improve the generation of effective counterspeech by overcoming the vagueness of zero-shot models.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: Focuses on token-oriented inference optimization, a critical area for making large models scalable and cost-effective.
Proposes a novel four-layer technical architecture: Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, Compute-Model Fusion, and Compute-Network-Model Fusion.
Provides a systematic review of key technologies and current industry status within these optimization layers.

stories.title: PsyScore: A Psychometrically-Aware Framework for Trait-Adaptive Essay Scoring and ZPD-Scaffolded Feedback
stories.author: Chanjin Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces PsyScore, a framework designed to improve Automated Essay Scoring (AES) by integrating psychological principles.
Addresses the limitation of current systems where scoring and feedback are treated separately, often lacking personalization.
The framework aims to provide both reliable assessment and actionable, proficiency-level-aware instructional feedback.

stories.title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
stories.author: Celestine Achi
bullet_points: Introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework (MIF), a nine-dimension schema for analyzing Nigerian public discourse.
Argues that current AI models fail on Nigerian languages due to 'context failure,' not just translation issues.
Critiques existing benchmarks (like NaijaSenti) for oversimplifying sentiment classification to a three-way polarity task.

stories.title: Codex logging bug may write TBs to local SSDs
stories.author: vantareed
bullet_points: A potential logging bug exists within Codex.
The bug has the capability to write massive amounts of data (TBs) to local SSDs.
This represents a significant security vulnerability in a major AI tool.

stories.title: When Software Started Writing Software: A Developer’s History of AI
stories.author: adamthedeveloper
bullet_points: The article discusses the historical shift in software development driven by AI.
It addresses the noticeable impact of AI on modern job descriptions for developers.
The content is framed as a personal reflection on the evolution of software creation.

stories.title: Phase 1: Document Ingestion - The Hidden Complexity Before Embeddings
stories.author: surajrkhonde
bullet_points: The article addresses a common failure point in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems: the initial document ingestion phase.
It highlights that the complexity often lies in preprocessing and ingestion, not just the embedding generation itself.
This suggests that robust RAG implementation requires deep attention to data quality and ingestion pipelines before advanced ML steps.
